rows.notes: Page title at top, clearly written and centered: 'Soluta Varia.' is legible and unambiguous.

rows.description: To Mr Farrand attending Nic: Price Day
rows.notes: Description mostly legible but surname uncertain (reads like 'Farrand' or 'Sarrand'); alignment of amounts with vertical column lines is clear. Pounds = 0, Shillings = 5, Pence = 0 unambiguous from right-hand columns.
Name 'Molineux' (or variant spelling) is legible. Numeric columns are clear and all values within valid ranges (9£ 8s 2d).
Given name initial and surname read as 'G Lambert' (could be 'Lumbart' in handwriting); amount 1£ 6s 0d lines up cleanly in the columns.
Description reads as '3 Corn Price' (could be '3 Corn Price' or similar). Amount 0£ 3s 0d is clearly aligned and valid.
Surname looks like 'Teasler' or 'Teaslers'. Numeric reading 14£ 0s 10d is clear; pence digit 10 is legible and within range.

rows.description: Fletchers Bill for 2 year Books & Prayer Books
rows.notes: Long description is legible: 'Fletchers Bill for 2 year Books & Prayer Books'. Amount 16£ 10s 6d clearly aligned and unambiguous.
Surname uncertain in handwriting (reads like 'Boltons' or 'Bolloni'); numeric columns 2£ 12s 6d are clear and valid. Chose 'Boltons' as most likely common name form.
Reads as 'Taggs Bill for Rectors garden' (could be 'Tagg's'). Amount 1£ 11s 0d lines up with shilling column and is clear.
Handwriting reads 'Cook's Bill for Dinner &c'. Amount 16£ 6s 2d is clear and well aligned in the £/s/d columns.
Name and purpose read 'Townsend Masons Bill'. Amount 7£ 15s 0d is clear and valid.
Reads 'Thomson Carpenters Bill'. Amount 17£ 18s 0d is clearly written and columns align well.
Description reads 'Townsend a Tax for lighting &c' (some abbreviation/wording uncertain). Amount 2£ 0s 0d is clearly placed in columns.

Sum line immediately under the list of 'Soluta Varia' entries; numeric cluster reads '49 16 2' aligned across the £/s/d columns. No textual label besides the horizontal rule, so description left blank.
Text to the left of the number reads 'last Page' (handwritten). Amount 77£ 5s 9d is clear and aligned. Relationship to previous total is structural (part of carry/aggregate).
Section header clearly written: 'Soluta Ordinaria' (distinct, centered-ish). No monetary amounts on this header line.

Abbreviated Latin label reads 'Act. schol. et Officiaries' (interpreted as 'Act. schol. & officiaries'). Amount 559£ 13s 7d is visible though ink slightly darker/fainter in places; digits align with the £/s/d column lines.

There is a prominent struck-through label (illegible due to cross-out). To the right the numeric total reads '1332 10 6' with a small quarter-pence mark (farthing) visible — interpreted as 0.25. Because the label is struck through the textual description is unreadable; numeric reading is reasonably clear but the cross-out reduces confidence.
Label 'Summa Receptorum' is legible. Numeric reading '1575 6 0' is clear and aligned; all currency columns within valid ranges.

Label 'Solutorum' (sum of payments) is clear. Amount repeated here '1332 10 6' with a visible farthing mark (interpreted as 0.25) matching the earlier struck-through numeric total; columns align well.

Reads 'Restant Debita a Dno Computante' (remaining debts to the gentleman computing). Numeric value at right appears as '242 15 5' with a three-quarter mark (three farthings, interpreted as 0.75). Slight smudge on fractional mark but shape consistent with '3q'.
Date line 'pro 21. Die Novembris 1775' is a clear datation/heading; no amounts on this line.
Phrase 'Computus comprobatus per Nos' (computation checked by us) is legible and clearly functions as a header/introduction to signatures.

First signature/endorsement line reads 'Tho Bray' with a following office word resembling 'Rectorum' or 'Rectorum'. Handwriting is cursive but legible enough; interpreted as a signature heading rather than a monetary row.
Second signature reads 'H. Richards' and the office looks like 'Subrectorem' (sub-rector). Penmanship is slightly lighter but readable.
Third signature appears 'Tho: Stinton' with an office reading 'Decanum' (dean). Slight uncertainty on surname spelling ('Stinton' plausible).

Signature reads something like 'Fran Upton' followed by abbreviated office which is difficult to parse (looks like 'Vicar' or 'Vary Sec.'). Handwriting light and abbreviations obscure exact office — included as header/signature with low confidence.

This signature line is heavily abbreviated and partly overwritten; reads approximately 'Jo: Sorrande' with 'Burs:' (bursar) following but both name and office are uncertain due to overwriting and faded ink.
Final signature looks like 'J. Toms' followed by heavy scribbles/abbreviations. The line is partly crossed and contains marginal annotations; read as a signature/header with low confidence.
